summary: Central Europe's largest economy, a key NATO eastern flank state, and now Europe's main conduit for military aid to Ukraine — building one of Europe's largest armies after centuries of being partitioned by neighbours.

history.detail: Mieszko I accepts Christianity; Poland enters European civilisation.
Poland disappears from map, divided between Russia, Prussia, Austria for 123 years.
Poland re-emerges after WWI; fights Soviet Russia 1920 (Battle of Warsaw).

history.event: WWII — invaded by Germany and USSR
history.detail: First country invaded in WWII; 6 million Polish citizens killed (half Jewish).
Heroic but failed 63-day uprising against Nazi occupation; city levelled.
Lech Wałęsa leads trade union that challenges Communist rule.
Roundtable Agreement; first free elections in Eastern Europe.
Economic transformation; largest single beneficiary of EU structural funds.

finance.currency_role: Złoty (PLN); retained own currency outside eurozone

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: CEE's largest; strong gaming and fintech

resources.oil_gas: Some domestic gas; coal-dependent economy transitioning
resources.coal: EU's largest coal producer; major political issue

follow_the_money.detail: 4% of GDP — highest in NATO; major arms purchases

theories_disputed.detail: Polish president Kaczyński and 95 officials killed; official: pilot error in fog; some politicians allege Russian involvement.

connections.detail: Permanent US troops stationed; Patriot missiles deployed
Main weapons transit and refugee host
